public_goals: Keep the child’s school and therapy plan stable enough to work in real life.
Avoid getting pushed into a partial agreement before the calendars and responsibilities line up.
Find a next step that acknowledges complexity without turning into endless delay.
private_concerns: Worries that agreeing to one element first will lock them into costs or schedule burdens they cannot sustain.
Feels the bundle is too interdependent to solve cleanly in one bounded AI-led pass.
Does not want a partial agreement later treated as proof that the rest should be easy.
red_lines: Will not accept a weekday or school change if therapy and transport feasibility remain unresolved.
Will not commit to expense-sharing language that stays vague once the rest of the bundle changes.
emotional_triggers: partial agreement pressure before costs and transport are clear
vague expense-sharing language presented as good enough
disclosure_tendencies: discloses strain more clearly once hidden burdens are acknowledged
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: pulls back if partial progress is overstated
engages more when a broader human review is framed as realism rather than failure
interest_profile: A next step that does not overclaim progress
starting_positions: Primary position: no single piece should be finalized before the school, therapy, transport, and cost bundle is viewed together.
Secondary position: if the bundle stays too interdependent, a human review is a better next step than a misleading partial package.
likely_openings: A partial answer here will make the whole thing look easier than it is.
The school and therapy timing changes are not independent from who drives and who pays.
